spec_match.rationale: Uses max-age= inside Use-As-Dictionary but the chrome 142 ship is ttl=. The page emits 'Use-As-Dictionary: match=..., id=v1, type=raw, max-age=2592000' which is not the parameter being added. The expiry logic IS correct in spirit but the header syntax is wrong and will mislead authors who copy-paste it.

interactive.rationale: Four range sliders drive a real two-bar comparison (brotli no-dict vs brotli + dict), a savings percentage with expired/active colouring, and a live header preview — well above the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature blurb sells 'bundle delta savings driven by dict TTL'. Page shows the savings curve nicely but the header it emits names max-age rather than the ttl parameter the feature actually adds, so it doesn't quite explain WHAT lever was newly added.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers fresh vs expired with a visible fallback-to-brotli state. Missing: ttl=0 instant-expire, change-rate 0% (perfect dictionary match — should asymptote toward header-only payload), very-large change rate where delta encoding is no better than brotli, and the Sec-Dictionary-Id round trip.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/6408307086589952 and the developer.chrome.com shared-dictionary-compression blog.

design_system.rationale: Uses --bg-*, --accent-emerald (fresh savings), --accent-rose (expired); no static-card pattern; range inputs styled via design system.

openQuestions.title: CRITICAL: switch Use-As-Dictionary header from max-age= to ttl=
openQuestions.detail: The whole point of the v142 feature is the new ttl= parameter on Use-As-Dictionary. This page generates 'Use-As-Dictionary: match=..., max-age=2592000' which is the wrong parameter name — the feature adds 'ttl=2592000'. The sibling concepts (dict-ttl, variant-explosion) use ttl= correctly. This is a copy-paste hazard — fix the header generation in the script so it emits ttl=<seconds> not max-age=<seconds>.

openQuestions.title: Add a Sec-Available-Dictionary / Content-Encoding: dcb round trip
openQuestions.detail: The 'relevant headers' block alludes to Sec-Available-Dictionary and Content-Encoding: dcb but the live simulator never shows them. Render a third panel that shows the subsequent versioned bundle request including the dictionary hash and the dcb response so authors see both sides of the negotiation.

openQuestions.title: Show the perfect-match floor and the high-change-rate ceiling
openQuestions.detail: Change-rate 0% (perfect match) should show savings asymptoting at near-100% — currently the floor is min 1%. Change-rate near 60% means delta encoding is barely better than brotli; the page doesn't surface that crossover. Both ends are useful for picking the deploy cadence story.

summary: Strong simulator with one critical bug: the generated Use-As-Dictionary header uses max-age= instead of the ttl= parameter the feature actually adds. Fix the script first; then expand to show the request side (Sec-Available-Dictionary) and the change-rate extremes.
